title: Customer Support Department Performance Indicators
content:
Customer Support Department Performance Indicators

Support Satisfaction (SSAT)

SSAT is very good (above 93%) but target is 95%. Collecting feedback from dissatisfied customers to identify trends. Support Managers review trends to create changes to workflows and/or training.

SSAT is very good (above 93%) but target is 95%.

Collecting feedback from dissatisfied customers to identify trends.

Support Managers review trends to create changes to workflows and/or training.

Manager to customer support rep ratio

While we have an overall ratio of less than 10:1 as a department at time of this evaluation, we have re-balanced the larger imbalance in EMEA with new management in place.

While we have an overall ratio of less than 10:1 as a department at time of this evaluation, we have re-balanced the larger imbalance in EMEA with new management in place.

Service Level Agreement (SLA)

Inconsistent performance to a 95% target achievement. Focused staffing to key time gaps with higher level of ticket SLA breaches. Adjusting workflow to enable working with customers in their preferred timezone. Working with the data team on understanding the inconsistent we see between our ZenDesk SLA data and Periscope via 2857

Inconsistent performance to a 95% target achievement.

Focused staffing to key time gaps with higher level of ticket SLA breaches.

Adjusting workflow to enable working with customers in their preferred timezone.

Working with the data team on understanding the inconsistent we see between our ZenDesk SLA data and Periscope via 2857

Customer Support Margin

Tracked and reported monthly, and for the year we are under our margin target for spend. Maintain focus on spend while iterating on needs for non-headcount expense requirements.

Key Performance Indicators

Support Satisfaction (SSAT)

A measure of how satisfied our customers’ are with their interaction with the GitLab Support team. This is based on survey responses from customers after each ticket is solved by the Support team using a Good or Bad rating.

Target: At or above 95% Health: Attention SSAT is very good (above 93%) but target is 95%. Collecting feedback from dissatisfied customers to identify trends. Support Managers review trends to create changes to workflows and/or training. Chart

SSAT is very good (above 93%) but target is 95%.

Collecting feedback from dissatisfied customers to identify trends.

Support Managers review trends to create changes to workflows and/or training.

Manager to customer support rep ratio

The Manager to IC Ratio is the ratio of individual contributors to one manager. This data comes from Bamboo HR.

Target: The target for this metric is at or below 10:1 Health: Okay While we have an overall ratio of less than 10:1 as a department at time of this evaluation, we have re-balanced the larger imbalance in EMEA with new management in place. Chart

While we have an overall ratio of less than 10:1 as a department at time of this evaluation, we have re-balanced the larger imbalance in EMEA with new management in place.

Service Level Agreement (SLA)

GitLab Support commits to an initial substantive response in a specified amount of time from the time the customer submits a ticket. The SLA for this first reply is based on each customer’s Support Plan.

Target: At or above 95% to Priority Support SLAs Health: Attention Inconsistent performance to a 95% target achievement. Focused staffing to key time gaps with higher level of ticket SLA breaches. Adjusting workflow to enable working with customers in their preferred timezone. Working with the data team on understanding the inconsistent we see between our ZenDesk SLA data and Periscope via 2857 Chart

Inconsistent performance to a 95% target achievement.

Focused staffing to key time gaps with higher level of ticket SLA breaches.

Adjusting workflow to enable working with customers in their preferred timezone.

Working with the data team on understanding the inconsistent we see between our ZenDesk SLA data and Periscope via 2857

https://about.gitlab.com/support/#service-level-agreement-sla-details

https://gitlab.com/gitlab-data/analytics/-/issues/2857

https://gitlab.com/gitlab-data/analytics/-/issues/2857

Data Privacy Requests - Service Level Agreement (SLA)

GitLab Support responds to Data Subject Requests in an amount of time required by the laws of various governing bodies. The SLA for these requests is determined by the legal team.

Target: At or above 95% SLAs Health: Attention Inconsistent performance to 30 day resolution targets Chart

Inconsistent performance to 30 day resolution targets

https://handbook.gitlab.com/handbook/support/workflows/account_deletion_access_request_workflows.html

Customer Support Margin

Total Support headcount and non-headcount expenses as a percent of ARR.

Target: Headcount and non-headcount expenses to be at or below 10% of ARR Health: Okay Tracked and reported monthly, and for the year we are under our margin target for spend. Maintain focus on spend while iterating on needs for non-headcount expense requirements.

Tracked and reported monthly, and for the year we are under our margin target for spend.

Maintain focus on spend while iterating on needs for non-headcount expense requirements.

The following KPI tracks the ratio between the median “Resolution Time” and the total median “Customer Wait Time” per ticket. “Resolution Time” is defined as the amount of time between the ticket creation and the last ticket communication. “Customer Wait Time” is defined as the total amount of time the ticket spends in the “Open” & “New” states over the tickets life cycle.

Target: At or below 35% Health: Okay TBD Chart

Support Handbook MR Rate

The handbook is essential to working remote successfully, to keeping up our transparency, and to recruiting successfully. Our processes are constantly evolving and we need a way to make sure the handbook is being updated at a regular cadence. This data is retrieved by querying the API with a python script for merge requests that have files matching /source/handbook/support/** over time.

Target: 0.5 Health: Okay above target Chart

Support Department MR Rate is not directly a key performance indicator used to indicate productivity of our Support team members. Nonetheless, we want to track the average MR merged per team member to encourage updates to Documentation and Support ‘fixes’. We currently count all members of the Support Department (Director, EMs, ICs) in the denominator because this is a team effort. The full definition of MR Rate is linked in the url section.

Target: At or above 1 MRs per Month Health: Problem TBD Chart

Support Team Member Retention

We need to be able to retain talented team members. Retention measures our ability to keep them sticking around at GitLab. Team Member Retention = (1-(Number of Team Members leaving GitLab/Average of the 12 month Total Team Member Headcount)) x 100. GitLab measures team member retention over a rolling 12 month period.

Target: at or above 84% This KPI cannot be public Health: Okay above target URL(s):

https://10az.online.tableau.com/#/site/gitlab/views/N5AttritionDashboard/AttritionDashboard?:iid=1

Support Average Age of Open Positions

Measures the average time job openings take from open to close. This metric includes sourcing time of candidates compared to Time to Hire or Time to Offer Accept which only measures the time from when a candidate applies to when they accept.

Target: at or below 50 days Unknown new metric, monitoring progress Chart

new metric, monitoring progress

Regular Performance Indicators

Support Performance to Operating Plan

Tracks overall Support spend against expectations from GitLab’s Operational Plan.

Target: at or within 5% of Fiscal Year Operating plan Unknown This is a new proposed PI Data is currently tracked in a spreadsheet.

This is a new proposed PI

Data is currently tracked in a spreadsheet.

Support Department Discretionary Bonus Rate

The number of discretionary bonuses given divided by the total number of team members, in a given period as defined. This metric definition is taken from the People Success Discretionary Bonuses KPI .

Target: at or above 10% Health: Attention Metric is new and is being monitored Chart

Metric is new and is being monitored

Support Department Promotion Rate

The total number of promotions over a rolling 12 month period divided by the month end headcount. The target promotion rate is 12% of the population. This metric definition is taken from the People Success Team Member Promotion Rate PI .

Target: 12% Health: Okay Metric is new and is being monitored Between Dec 2019 and Nov 2020, we had a one time change for a group of Service Agents to Support Engineers. Due to this change, the promotion rate is higher in those months. Aug 2020 through Nov 2020 have been corrected to reflect this. Chart

Metric is new and is being monitored

Between Dec 2019 and Nov 2020, we had a one time change for a group of Service Agents to Support Engineers. Due to this change, the promotion rate is higher in those months. Aug 2020 through Nov 2020 have been corrected to reflect this.

The KPI is at an acceptable level compared to the threshold

This is a blip, or we’re going to watch it, or we just need to enact a proven intervention

We'll prioritize our efforts here

Metric & metric health are confidential

How pages like this work

The heart of pages like this are Performance Indicators data files which are YAML files.
Each - denotes a dictionary of values for a new (K)PI. The current elements (or data properties) are:

String value of the name of the (K)PI. For Product PIs, product hierarchy should be separate from name by " - " (Ex. {Stage Name}:{Group Name} - {PI Type} - {PI Name}

Relative path to the performance indicator page that this (K)PI should live on

refer to Parts of a KPI

should be used when a (K)PI is a subset of another PI. For example, we might care about Hiring vs Plan at the company level. The child would be the division and department levels, which would have the parent flag.

The target or cap for the (K)PI. Please use Unknown until we reach maturity level 2 if this is not yet defined. For GMAU, the target should be quarterly.

the organizational grouping (Ex: Engineering Function or Development Department). For Product Sections, ensure you have the word section (Ex : Dev Section)

the product section (Ex: dev) as defined in sections.yml

the product stage (Ex: release) as defined in stages.yml

the product group (Ex: progressive_delivery) as defined in stages.yml

the product group (Ex: feature_flags) as defined in categories.yml

boolean value (true/false) that indicates if it is a (key) performance indicator

indicates the (K)PI health and reasons as nested attributes. This should be updated monthly before Key Reviews by the DRI.

indicates a value between 0 and 3 (inclusive) to represent the health of the (K)PI. This should be updated monthly before Key Reviews by the DRI.

indicates the reasons behind the health level. This should be updated monthly before Key Reviews by the DRI. Should be an array (indented lines starting with dashes) even if you only have one reason.

list of urls associated with the (K)PI. Should be an array (indented lines starting with dashes) even if you only have one url

indicates there is a handbook link for a description of the funnel for this PI. Should be a URL

boolean flag that can be set to false where a (K)PI does not meet the public guidelines.

indicates the Product PI type (Ex: AMAU, GMAU, SMAU, Group PPI)

product_analytics_type

indicates if the metric is available on SaaS, SM (self-managed), or Both.

boolean flag that indicates if this is the Primary PI for the Product Group.

indicates the implementation status and reasons as nested attributes. This should be updated monthly before Key Reviews by the DRI.

implementation.status

indicates the Implementation Status status. This should be updated monthly before Key Reviews by the DRI.

implementation.reasons

indicates the reasons behind the implementation status. This should be updated monthly before Key Reviews by the DRI. Should be an array (indented lines starting with dashes) even if you only have one reason.

indicates lessons learned from a K(PI) as a nested attribute. This should be updated monthly before Key Reviews by the DRI.

learned is an attribute that can be nested under lessons and indicates lessons learned from a K(PI). This should be updated monthly before Key Reviews by the DRI. Should be an array (indented lines starting with dashes) even if you only have one lesson learned

indicates monthly focus goals from a K(PI) as a nested attribute. This should be updated monthly before Key Reviews by the DRI.

indicates monthly focus goals from a K(PI). This should be updated monthly before Key Reviews by the DRI. Should be an array (indented lines starting with dashes) even if you only have one goal

indicates the name of the metric in Self-Managed implemenation. The SaaS representation of the Self-Managed implementation should use the same name.
